symbols: ropes and tassels — binding and devotion, the cords of faith and cultural tradition
dove pin on the woman's chest — the Holy Spirit and peace
word 'tierra' (earth) — the grounded, incarnational aspect of Marian devotion
profile of a woman — the enduring feminine icon of the Madonna across cultures
description: A mixed-media collage composition framed with ropes, tassels, and decorative elements. At center-left, a profile image of a woman with a yellow head covering and a small white dove pin at her chest. The word 'tierra' appears in an oval medallion. Various stamps, medallions, and decorative ornamental objects are arranged around the composition. Thick braided ropes and ornamental tassels form the border. The palette is warm with reds, golds, and yellows against a cream-colored center.
theological_description: Gironella's Madonna deconstructs and reconstructs the Marian image through a Latin American cultural lens, using collage, found objects, and popular imagery. While this is an artistic reinterpretation rather than a direct biblical scene, it engages with the enduring power of the Madonna as a cultural and spiritual icon. The word 'tierra' (earth) connects Mary to the soil and land — she is the humble earth from which the fruit of salvation sprang. Luke records Mary's own words: 'He has regarded the humble estate of his servant' (Luke 1:48). The dove at her breast recalls the Holy Spirit who overshadowed her at the Annunciation: 'The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you' (Luke 1:35). The collage technique itself — assembling diverse cultural fragments into a unified devotional image — mirrors how Marian devotion has organically incorporated the cultural materials of every people who has received the Gospel, from the Black Madonnas of Europe to Our Lady of Guadalupe in the Americas.

scripture_refs.reason: Mary's humble estate regarded by God — the grounded, earthy quality of this Madonna reflects her lowliness
The Holy Spirit overshadowing Mary — symbolized by the dove pinned to the figure's chest
Elizabeth's proclamation of Mary as blessed among women — the cultural veneration this collage represents
